Area boys.description: Loosely organized gangs of street children, teenagers, and youths, mostly male, operating in major Nigerian cities. They engage in extortion, illegal drug sales, and informal security. Originating from colonial-era groups like Jaguda boys and Boma boys, they remain active in modern Nigeria.

No Surrender Motorcycle Club.description: International one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club established in the Netherlands in 2013 by Klaas Otto, a former Satudarah member. Claims over 1,600 members worldwide, with leadership primarily of Dutch Traveller, Turkish, and Kurdish origin. Banned in the Netherlands since 2022, but operates in multiple countries including Canada, Australia, and Europe.

'Ndrina Faillace.description: The 'Ndrina Faillace is a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Cassano all'Ionio. It operates in the Sibari Plain and the province of Cosenza, belonging to the Locale di Sibari. The clan emerged victorious from a feud with the Abruzzese-Pepe of Lauropoli alongside the Forastefano and Portoraro clans. In October 2009, lands, cars, and companies belonging to the Faillace were confiscated.

Chiapas–Guatemala Cartel.description: Mexican drug cartel active in Chiapas and parts of Guatemala, founded in 2022 as a splinter group of the Sinaloa Cartel. It emerged after a betrayal by Juan Manuel Valdovinos Mendoza and was founded by Baldemar Calderón Carrillo. The cartel is involved in drug trafficking across the Guatemala-Mexico border and has published execution videos. It is allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and rivals the Sinaloa Cartel.

Pietraperzia Mafia family.description: The Pietraperzia Mafia family is a Cosa Nostra crime family based in Pietraperzia, Sicily, with roots dating to the early 1900s. It reemerged in the 1980s under Liborio Micciché and later Giovanni Monachino, becoming a key ally of the Catania Mafia family. The family was involved in high-level operations, including preparatory meetings for the 1992 Capaci bombing, and was targeted in the large-scale 'Operazione Leopardo' crackdown. It remains active as of the present.

Sinaloa Cartel.description: Mexican transnational criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illicit activities. Based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, it was founded in 1989 by Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, and others. Considered the most important cartel in Mexico and one of the most influential globally, it controls major drug routes into the United States and operates in multiple continents. Currently divided into factions led by Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar (Los Chapitos) and Ismael Zambada Sicairos (La Mayiza).

'Ndrina Zindato.description: The Zindato-Caracciolo are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, allied with the more powerful Libri clan. Key figures include Francesco Zindato, arrested in 2010 for murder, and Antonino Caracciolo, arrested in 1999. The group has been involved in extortion and mafia association, with recent operations in 2010, 2014, and 2020 targeting its members.

Clan Cappello.description: The Clan Cappello is a Mafia organization and family originating from the province of Catania, Sicily, named after boss Salvatore Cappello. It is considered, together with the Santapaola family, one of the most powerful Cosa Nostra clans in eastern Sicily. The clan emerged from the Ferlito-Pillera group in the 1980s and has been involved in internal conflicts and alliances with other local clans.

Guadalajara Cartel.description: The Guadalajara Cartel was the first major Mexican drug trafficking organization, formed around 1978 by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, Rafael Caro Quintero, and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo. Based in Guadalajara, Jalisco, it controlled most drug trafficking routes in Mexico during the late 1970s and 1980s, smuggling opium, marijuana, and cocaine into the United States. The cartel was dismantled in 1989 following the arrests of its leaders.

Palestinian Islamic Jihad.description: Islamist Palestinian paramilitary organization formed in 1981 as an offshoot of the Muslim Brotherhood. It seeks the establishment of an Islamic Palestinian state through military means and rejects the Oslo Accords and two-state solution. Its armed wing, Al-Quds Brigades, operates in the West Bank and Gaza Strip, conducting suicide bombings and rocket attacks. Designated a terrorist organization by multiple countries.

Nuova camorra pugliese.description: The Nuova camorra pugliese was a criminal organization based in Apulia, Italy, active from the early 1970s to the mid-1980s. It was founded by Raffaele Cutolo as an offshoot of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, operating mainly in the provinces of Foggia and Taranto. The organization was eventually absorbed by the Sacra corona unita.

descriptions: Modeo and Vuto later aligned with Nuova Camorra Pugliese after Taranto fell under Camorra territory.
edges.evidence_quote: Tramite un accordo, Taranto rientrò poi nel territorio camorrista, e ciò spinse Modeo e Vuto ad accostarsi alla Nuova Camorra Pugliese voluta da Raffaele Cutolo.

Goto-gumi.description: The Goto-gumi is a Japanese yakuza organization based in Fujinomiya, Shizuoka, founded in 1985 by Tadamasa Goto. It operates as a secondary affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi, Japan's largest yakuza syndicate. The group was involved in various criminal activities, and its founder was a prominent crime boss in Tokyo. The organization's status after Goto's expulsion in 2008 and death in 2026 is unclear.

edges.evidence_quote: Michele Zaza, camorrista-contrabbandiere che divenne “uomo d’onore” per i siciliani e snodo dei traffici di “bionde” tra Tirreno e Napoli.

descriptions: Zaza and Bardellino (Casalesi) cooperated in cocaine distribution.

Charles Martel Group.description: French far-right anti-Arab militant group active in the 1970s and 1980s. Formed by veterans of the Organisation armée secrète, it targeted Algerian properties, businesses, and government offices. The group claimed responsibility for several bombings, including the 1973 Algerian consulate bombing in Marseille. Its status is inactive, with no members ever arrested.

descriptions: Charles Martel Group was formed by veterans of the Organisation armée secrète.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was formed by veterans of the Organisation armée secrète, which fought against Algerian independence during the Algerian War.
